_note: Original memtrust fixture -- not derived from any published dataset. Each case stores several records under one session, each tagged with a `ranking_field` metadata value, then queries the session and checks whether the returned order actually correlates with that field. Case mt-rank-001 and mt-rank-004 reproduce the exact mempalace/mempalace#1733 shape (GitHub user Kartalops): a ranking-relevant field that is either present-but-constant or entirely absent on every record, so any apparent ordering has no real per-record signal behind it. Case mt-rank-002 is the negative control: genuinely varied importance values that a correctly-behaving backend orders by descending value -- this must NOT be flagged. Case mt-rank-003 is a backend that has a real varying signal but doesn't order by it. Case mt-rank-005 models MemPalace/mempalace PR#1890's `authored_at` timestamp tie-breaker: two records tied on `hybrid_score` (present in metadata for realism, not itself the declared ranking_field) but differentiated only by `authored_at` -- the newer record (larger epoch value) must rank first. See docs/methodology.md and evals/ranking_quality.py for the full scoring logic.

cases.query: wake me up with my recent important memories

records.content: Had coffee with Alex near the office.
Signed the lease for the new apartment.
Grandmother's health scare, went to the hospital.
